# meetdelai.com — Edwin De Leon

**Site:** https://meetdelai.com
**Brand:** DELAI (DeLeonAI)
**Operator:** Edwin De Leon
**Location:** South Florida

---

## Who

Edwin De Leon is the founder of DELAI (DeLeonAI). Senior operator and builder who designs, codes, ships, and supports every product in the DELAI portfolio. Not a consulting firm — DELAI owns and runs each product end-to-end.

DELAI = a portfolio of standalone AI products that share an intelligence layer (Found), a data layer (Supabase), and an infrastructure layer (Mastra agent runtime on Hostinger + EC2).

---

## Portfolio

### Found — Personal AI
The intelligence layer for the portfolio. Tracks every project, person, decision, and signal across the entire DELAI ecosystem so context never gets lost between sessions. Powers the auto-memory and wiki ingest that the operator and the agents both consume.

### Modus — Operator AI
Productized AI automations sold as engines: $2.5k setup + $500/mo. Each engine = one deployed workflow for one customer, single-tenant, with explicit goal, KPIs, and channels. Engine #1 (aibiz-lead-triage) is live. Engine #2 (article generator) proved the factory pattern.

### BiTES — Food intelligence
Consumer AI food tracker. Plate-photo nutrition analysis (vision agent), AI coach Lily (Groq llama-3.3), restaurant menu scanning, USDA-grounded recipe generator. Live on iOS and Android via Capacitor. Stripe-monetized free + Pro tier.

### HostGPT — Short-term rental copilot
AI host for STR properties. Voice + chat + ops. White-labeled per property. Multi-agent orchestration across guest comms, cleaning ops, maintenance dispatch.

### PAGE — Micro-merchant payments
Stripe Connect Express acceptance layer for micro-merchants who don't want a full POS. Onboarding coach agent + support copilot + ops copilot. Auto-PR GitHub App for L3 incident resolution.

### Munchies — Food delivery
WhatsApp-first ordering for restaurants in Puerto Rico. Uber Direct certified. Twilio sandbox + production WhatsApp flows. Multi-location restaurant onboarding.

---

## How I work

### Discipline reachable from one place
Every agent flows through one Mastra registry. Every product writes to one Supabase. Every closeout updates one wiki. The gold-standard library is worthless if a wrapper sits next to it. When auditing, the question isn't "does it have a system prompt?" — it's "is the discipline reachable from one place?"

### Build the sandbox first
Never gate on Apple entitlements, live API keys, custom domains, or vendor approvals. Mock the integration boundary and ship. The real key arrives after the demo works.

### Four PRs, one A-grade
PR-D for the harness first, then PR-A/B/C for capability. Closed-loop validation. Telemetry from day one. Audits drive the plan, not the other way around.

### Y-Elmer audit on every initiative
Rate it against 7 YC AI-native principles before scoping. If the model is the product's only moat, the product is wrong.

### Stack defaults
- Frontend: React 19 + Vite 7 + TypeScript + Tailwind 3
- Backend: Mastra (Hostinger for DELAI, EC2 for MCM products)
- DB: Supabase
- LLM: Groq (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) by default; GPT-4o for vision only
- Media: fal.ai by default for image + video gen
- Schedulers: Hostinger cron (`187.124.64.14`)

---

## Engagements

I take a small number of engagements at a time. Three shapes:

- **Advisory** — recurring sessions for founders building AI-native products. Architecture, hiring, build/buy calls, agent design.
- **Fractional CTO** — embedded for 1–3 months to set up the engineering foundation (registry, agent layer, deploy harness) so the team can ship without me.
- **Full build** — DELAI builds and ships the product, hands off (or runs it via Modus).

Pricing happens in conversation — every engagement scopes to the specific problem.
